message:
type: SURVEY
files: dspy/propose/grounded_proposer.py
note: GroundedProposer.__init__ (add tip_weights param) and the tip-selection block inside propose_instructions_for_program (the 'if self.set_tip_randomly:' block, lines ~361-371), plus new helper method _select_tip_with_weights()

type: SURVEY
files: dspy/propose/grounded_proposer.py
note: My feature (cache-salt/no-temp-modulation) touches: propose_instruction_for_predictor (removing temperature modulation block), propose_instructions_for_program (call site), generate_instruction_class (adding cache_salt InputField to GenerateSingleModuleInstruction), DescribeProgram class (adding cache_salt field), DescribeModule class (adding cache_salt field), GenerateModuleInstruction.forward (passing cache_salt to describe_program/describe_module/generate_module_instruction calls), and new GroundedProposer._cache_salt method. What does your feature touch?

type: SURVEY
files: dspy/propose/grounded_proposer.py
note: My feature (weighted tip selection): touches GroundedProposer.__init__ (add tip_weights param), the tip-selection block inside propose_instructions_for_program (lines ~360-371, the 'if self.set_tip_randomly:' block), and adds new method _select_tip_with_weights(). Overlap: we both edit propose_instructions_for_program (you edit the call site to propose_instruction_for_predictor around lines 373-380, I edit the tip-selection block just above it) - let's co-draft the full method. Do you also add params to __init__ (e.g. cache_salt)?

type: DRAFT
files: dspy/propose/grounded_proposer.py
region: none - no overlapping constructs
note: Comparing surveys: your edits are GroundedProposer.__init__ (tip_weights param) + tip-selection block (lines ~361-371) + new _select_tip_with_weights(). Mine are propose_instruction_for_predictor body, generate_instruction_class, DescribeProgram, DescribeModule, GenerateModuleInstruction.forward, and new _cache_salt() method. These don't touch the same lines. To avoid insertion-point collisions for our new methods: I'll add _cache_salt() as a new method placed directly after __init__ ends and BEFORE the 'def propose_instructions_for_program' line. Could you place _select_tip_with_weights() elsewhere, e.g. directly after propose_instructions_for_program (before propose_instruction_for_predictor), so our insertions land at different anchor lines? Also FYI I will add a short comment next to the T parameter in both propose_instructions_for_program and propose_instruction_for_predictor signatures noting it's unused now - these are single-line comment additions on the existing 'T, # noqa: N803' lines, not behavioral changes to your tip block. Let me know if that works.

type: AGREE
files: dspy/propose/grounded_proposer.py
note: Agreed: no overlapping constructs. I will place _select_tip_with_weights() directly after propose_instructions_for_program (before propose_instruction_for_predictor). You place _cache_salt() after __init__ before propose_instructions_for_program. Your single-line comment additions on 'T, # noqa: N803' lines are fine and won't conflict with my tip-selection block edits (lines ~361-371). Proceeding to implement.

type: DRAFT
files: dspy/propose/grounded_proposer.py
region: propose_instructions_for_program - NO changes needed from me, false alarm
note: Correction: on closer look, I don't need to touch propose_instructions_for_program at all. My cache_salt is computed INSIDE propose_instruction_for_predictor from its existing pred_i/demo_set_i params (already passed in unchanged), so the call site at lines ~373-383 stays byte-identical - no cache_salt param added there. I also do NOT add any param to GroundedProposer.__init__ (no cache_salt param; I reuse the existing self.rng). So propose_instructions_for_program (signature, tip-selection block, and the call-site loop) is entirely yours to edit - no co-drafting needed there. My only edits are: (1) new self._cache_salt() method on GroundedProposer (I'll place it right after __init__, before propose_instructions_for_program - let me know if that collides with where you insert _select_tip_with_weights), (2) inside propose_instruction_for_predictor: remove the original_temp/epsilon/modified_temp block, add cache_salt computation + pass to instruction_generator call, (3) generate_instruction_class + DescribeProgram + DescribeModule: add cache_salt InputField, (4) GenerateModuleInstruction.forward: accept/pass cache_salt through the three calls. None of this touches your tip_weights or tip-selection code. Sound good?

type: AGREE
files: dspy/propose/grounded_proposer.py
note: Confirmed, no collision: your _cache_salt() goes right after __init__ (before propose_instructions_for_program), my _select_tip_with_weights() goes right after propose_instructions_for_program ends (before propose_instruction_for_predictor). I've already implemented tip_weights param in __init__, the tip-selection block, and _select_tip_with_weights() in propose_instructions_for_program - untouched by your cache_salt work. Sounds good, proceeding.

type: DONE
files: dspy/propose/grounded_proposer.py
note: Finished tip_weights feature: __init__ param, tip-selection block, _select_tip_with_weights() helper placed after propose_instructions_for_program. No further changes planned on my end.

type: DONE
files: dspy/propose/grounded_proposer.py
note: Finished cache-salt feature: removed temperature modulation in propose_instruction_for_predictor, added _cache_salt() helper after __init__, added cache_salt InputField to DescribeProgram/DescribeModule/GenerateSingleModuleInstruction signatures, threaded cache_salt through GenerateModuleInstruction.forward's three calls. Tests pass (tests/propose/test_grounded_proposer.py). No further changes planned on my end.
